name: Red Pepper Cheesecakes
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C).
2. Combine breadcrumbs, butter, and salt in a bowl; mix well.
3. Press the mixture onto the bottom and up the sides of a mini muffin pan.
4. In another bowl, combine cream cheese, scallions, jack cheese, and half and half. Mix well.
5. Beat in eggs one at a time, then set aside.
6. Prepare a small bed of coals.
7. Soak hickory chips in water for 15-30 minutes, drain well.
8. Place the soaked hickory chips onto the hot coals.
9. Grill half red peppers above and away from the coals.
10. Smoke the peppers for 10-15 minutes.
11. Dice the smoked peppers.
12. Divide among the prepared crusts.
13. Pour the egg mixture into the prepared crusts.
14. Bake at 325°F (165°C) until lightly browned on top, about 20-25 minutes.
15. Remove from oven and let cool completely.
16. In a small saucepan or skillet, combine red pepper puree and corn syrup over medium-low heat.
17. Mix cornstarch and water in a separate bowl; whisk into the puree.
18. Add to the pan and stir until well thickened.
19. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
20. Spoon a small amount of the sweetened mixture on top of each cheesecake.
21. Serve warm or at room temperature.
